title: NASA FIRMS satellite imagery confirms fires at Perm oil refinery and pumping station following Ukrainian strikes
summary: Satellite thermal detection via NASA FIRMS captured fire signatures at both the Perm oil-pumping station and Lukoil refinery following Ukrainian drone attacks, providing independent verification of strike damage.
contents:
# Satellite Imagery Confirms Perm Refinery Fire Signatures

## Detection Overview
NASA's Fire Information for Resource Management System (FIRMS) detected thermal anomalies at two locations in the Perm region of Russia on April 30-May 1, 2026, independently confirming Ukrainian claims of successful drone strikes on energy infrastructure.

## Satellite Observations
- Thermal hotspots detected at the Perm oil-pumping station coordinates
- Additional fire signatures identified in the vicinity of the Lukoil Permnefteorgsintez refinery
- MODIS and VIIRS instruments aboard multiple satellite platforms captured the data
- Bloomberg published satellite imagery confirming the fire locations

## Intelligence Value
- Satellite-based damage assessment provides independent verification of strike claims
- All-weather SAR and thermal imaging capabilities enable persistent monitoring regardless of cloud cover
- Change detection algorithms track infrastructure status over time
- The observations demonstrate the role of commercial satellite intelligence in conflict monitoring

## Broader Context
Commercial satellite imagery continues to play a critical role in verifying military operations in the Ukraine-Russia conflict. The accessibility of platforms like NASA FIRMS enables open-source intelligence analysts to independently assess damage claims from both sides.
